Patch Set 2:

Is this the best approach to this problem?

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

> Is this the best approach to this problem?

It is a reasonable approach, in my opinion. What I like about it is that it doesn't require setting the value separately for each interface. (Being able to set these kinds of values per interface is a very nice feature to have. Being required to do so is not.)

One concern I have is that divide-by-two can give you values that are almost half of the highest reasonable value. Would a more gradual decrease be feasible? (For example: if the original is > 2k, remove 1k and try again, if the original > 256, remove 128 and try again, if the original > 16, remove 8 and try again.)

parents.subject: LU-7003 utils: must quote the value of the context option

commit.subject: LU-7124 o2iblnd: limit cap.max_send_wr for MLX5
commit.message:
LU-7124 o2iblnd: limit cap.max_send_wr for MLX5

Decrease cap.max_send_wr until it is accepted by rdma_create_qp()

Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ib76f07d997ea579f86fca467329ad357ed26b36f
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/18347
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Olaf Weber <olaf@sgi.com>
Reviewed-by: Doug Oucharek <doug.s.oucharek@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
